content:
# Dhruma Formation

**Summary**: A Middle Jurassic carbonate formation.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] studied the dhruma-formation to understand its stratigraphic position and members.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] studied the Dhruma Formation, specifically the D4 and D5 members.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] studied the Dhruma Formation to characterize its reservoir properties and lithofacies.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] studied the Dhruma Formation to characterize its microfacies and reservoir properties.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] studied the Dhruma Formation to evaluate reservoir quality.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] analyed the Dhurma formation for porosity and permeability.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] studied the Dhruma Formation to characterize its lower units (D1-D2).

- [[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024]] studied the Dhruma Formation's D7 unit (Atash and Hisyan Members).

## Depositional Environment

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] moderately shallow lagoonal setting

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] studied the Dhruma Formation, specifically the D4 and D5 members.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] studied the Dhruma Formation to characterize its reservoir properties and lithofacies.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] studied the Dhruma Formation to characterize its microfacies and reservoir properties.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] studied the Dhruma Formation to evaluate reservoir quality.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] analyed the Dhurma formation for porosity and permeability.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] studied the Dhruma Formation to characterize its lower units (D1-D2).

- [[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024]] studied the Dhruma Formation's D7 unit (Atash and Hisyan Members).

## Reservoir Properties



## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Dhruma Formation is divided into three units: lower, middle, and upper Dhruma. ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 27]])

- The Dhruma Formation contains 7 members (D2-D7) and is of Bajocian to Bathonian age. ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 1]])

- The Dhruma Formation contains several members (D2-D7) with varying petrophysical characteristics. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 23]])

- The formation is a good example of an ancient carbonate ramp system with three distinct members. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 16]])

- The formation is divided into D5 and D6 units with distinct lithofacies and diagenetic histories. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 1]])

- Dhurma mudstone showed spicules and moldic porosity. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 68]])

- The formation is of Bajocian to Bathonian age and contains a high diversity of small benthic foraminifera in its lower parts. ([[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022, p. 10]])

- The D7 unit shows distinct transgressive-regressive cycles. ([[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024, p. 15]])
